message:
feat(frankenpandas): add ToDatetimeOptions + ToDatetimeOrigin to prelude (fd90.211 / br-wfgld)

Same half-surface defect family as fd90.207/209/210:

- to_datetime_with_options is in the prelude
- BUT ToDatetimeOptions and ToDatetimeOrigin (its required parameter
  types) were exposed at the top-level only

User code that wants to call:
    to_datetime_with_options(series, &ToDatetimeOptions {
        format: Some("%Y-%m-%d"),
        origin: ToDatetimeOrigin::Int(0),
        ..Default::default()
    })?

cannot resolve ToDatetimeOptions/ToDatetimeOrigin from the prelude
alone, even though the consuming function is in the prelude.

Fix:
- Add ToDatetimeOptions, ToDatetimeOrigin to crate::prelude
- Extend prelude_completeness_compile_guard. Both types carry a
  lifetime 'a, so used `ToDatetimeOptions<'_>` and the lifetime-free
  `ToDatetimeOrigin::Int(0)` variant.

Verified: 35 integration tests + 7 lib tests + 2 doctests pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -862,6 +862,7 @@
 {"id":"frankenpandas-we6ql","title":"fd90.146: lock in README Quick Example via integration test (regression guard for prelude)","description":"fd90.74-145 README audit. The fd90.121-fd90.144 sequence expanded the frankenpandas prelude to cover the README's documented API surface. The 6 unit tests inside src/lib.rs (prelude_smoke_test, prelude_concat_works, prelude_query_works, prelude_io_roundtrip, prelude_to_datetime_works, rusqlite_reexport_quickstart_compiles) exercise individual prelude items but not the END-TO-END Quick Example as a single script.

Without a single integration test that runs the README's Quick Example top-to-bottom, future prelude changes (or accidental removals) wouldn't surface a regression until users complain.

Fix: add crates/frankenpandas/tests/readme_quick_example.rs (an integration test, NOT in src/lib.rs) that mirrors the README's Quick Example block at lines 41-63, plus the more elaborate Quick Start at lines 209-234, exercising:
  read_csv_str, query, groupby, agg_named,
  write_json_string, write_feather_bytes,
  Series::from_values, to_datetime,
  write_csv_string, write_sql, read_sql_table, SqlIfExists,
  frankenpandas::rusqlite::Connection::open_in_memory.

Verify with cargo test -p frankenpandas (default features, sql-sqlite enabled). The test must compile only via 'use frankenpandas::prelude::*' to lock in prelude completeness.

If the README example evolves, the test must too — or the README is lying.

Pure additive test. No behavior change.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"test","created_at":"2026-04-26T13:55:16.374371595Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T13:58:05.930614166Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T13:58:05.927845775Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-weh1","title":"[LOW] community: no .github/FUNDING.yml — no Sponsor button / funding channels declared","description":"GitHub Sponsors / funding configuration audit.

**Observation.** `ls .github/FUNDING.yml` → No such file. No declared funding channels (GitHub Sponsors / Open Collective / Liberapay / etc.).

**Context.** For an open-source library targeting a sizable user base (pandas has tens of millions of users; frankenpandas targets their Rust-curious subset), a documented funding mechanism is a cheap way to let engaged users contribute financially. GitHub renders a "Sponsor" button on the repo header when `FUNDING.yml` is present; without it, the button is absent and engaged users have no path.

**Severity.** LOW — no operational impact. No release blocker. No code quality issue. Filing truthfully as LOW; user-visible polish, optional feature.

**Fix shape.** `.github/FUNDING.yml`:
```yaml
# Map the repo's "Sponsor" button to one or more funding platforms.
# Each line enables a platform. Remove entries that don't apply.

github: [<org-or-user>]           # GitHub Sponsors
# open_collective: frankenpandas
# liberapay: <name>
# ko_fi: <name>
# custom: ["https://frankenpandas.dev/sponsor"]
```

Requires a decision: does the maintainer have an active GitHub Sponsors account? If yes, one-line file. If not, skip this bead entirely.

Blast radius: 1 tiny file (< 10 lines), zero code changes.

Prerequisites: Maintainer decides which funding channel(s) to expose. Nothing else.

Related:
- br-8k1i (SECURITY.md), br-6d5s (CONTRIBUTING.md) — same .github/ polish tier.
- No interlocks with other beads.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T22:18:55.853913193Z","created_by":"cc-pandas","updated_at":"2026-04-23T17:51:58.138001Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T17:51:58.138001Z","close_reason":".github/FUNDING.yml already shipped on main.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["community","funding","review"]}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-wfd4","title":"Implement pandas GroupBy sort= parity in fp-groupby","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T18:48:27.223493328Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T19:00:58.081046532Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T19:00:58.079013976Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"frankenpandas-wfgld","title":"fd90.211: Add ToDatetimeOptions + ToDatetimeOrigin to prelude","description":"fd90.211: Add ToDatetimeOptions + ToDatetimeOrigin to the prelude.

Half-surface defect (same family as fd90.207, fd90.209, fd90.210):

- to_datetime_with_options is in the prelude (line 53)
- BUT ToDatetimeOptions and ToDatetimeOrigin (its required parameter types) are exposed at the top-level (lines 36-38) but NOT in the prelude

User code following the README that calls `to_datetime_with_options(series, &ToDatetimeOptions { origin: ToDatetimeOrigin::Unix, ... })?` cannot resolve the option type names from `use frankenpandas::prelude::*;` alone.

Scope:
- Add ToDatetimeOptions, ToDatetimeOrigin to the prelude
- Extend prelude_completeness_compile_guard

Files: crates/frankenpandas/src/lib.rs.
Time estimate: 10-15min.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T22:05:33.586645298Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T22:07:41.985579060Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T22:07:41.983600584Z","close_reason":"Added ToDatetimeOptions + ToDatetimeOrigin to the prelude. Same half-surface defect as fd90.207/209/210 — to_datetime_with_options was in the prelude but its ToDatetimeOptions/ToDatetimeOrigin parameter types were only at top-level. Both have lifetime parameter 'a (ToDatetimeOptions has format/unit Option<&str>, ToDatetimeOrigin has Str(&str)/Int(i64)/Float(f64) variants — used Int(0) for the compile guard since it's lifetime-free). Extended prelude_completeness_compile_guard. All 35 integration tests + 7 lib tests + 2 doctests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-wgth","title":"fd90.75: README stale - 788 methods (actual 964) + all green claim contradicts 5 known failures","description":"Continuation of fd90.74 README audit. Two more stale numeric/factual claims: (1) line 125 says fp-frame has 788 methods - actual count of pub fn lines in fp-frame/src/lib.rs is 964. (2) Lines 37 and 869 claim conformance is all currently green under cargo test -p fp-conformance - actually 5 pre-existing tests fail (live_oracle_unavailable_falls_back_to_fixture_when_enabled, packet_filter_runs_csv_read_frame_parse_dates_mixed_timezone_packet, packet_filter_runs_dataframe_concat_axis1_packet, packet_filter_runs_dataframe_to_json_records_packet, fuzz_seed_fixtures_fuzz_json_io_bytes_accepts_records_seed_fixture). Update method count to current; soften all green claim to mention the small known-failing set so users running cargo test see expected behavior. Pure README change.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"docs","assignee":"cc-pandas-2","created_at":"2026-04-26T07:21:46.528434217Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T07:24:11.077891267Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T07:24:11.075275966Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["docs","fd90","readme","review"]}
